title: Automated decision & profiling (3) — European Data Protection Board (EDPB)
date_note: Publication-ready preview. Date requires final verification from source material.
summary: European Data Protection Board (EDPB) has published material relating to privacy biometrics. Source verification confirms official authority and accessibility. This record reflects metadata detected during regulatory monitoring. Organisations should review the original source material for detailed guidance. This is not legal advice.
evidence_required: Evidence of regulatory guidance monitoring process
Record of internal impact assessment against this guidance
Documentation of AI system inventory and applicable controls
Board or senior management sign-off on compliance posture
lessons: Regulatory bodies such as European Data Protection Board (EDPB) issue guidance that directly affects AI system deployments. Organisations must maintain active monitoring processes to detect and respond to new guidance before it becomes an enforcement trigger.
ai_system_context: AI governance case from EU jurisdiction
impact: Organisations using AI systems in EU face regulatory scrutiny from European Data Protection Board (EDPB). Failure to track and implement guidance from this source may result in enforcement action, fines, or operational restrictions. Relevant to AI procurement, deployment, and compliance programmes.

_preview_metadata.approval_required: Control Tower explicit approval needed before publication
